footballers.name: Wes Morgan
clues: I was born on the 21st of January 1984. I played 30 times for my country, never scoring a goal.
I'm the first Jamaican ever to win a Premier League medal — and the first to score in a Champions League game (against Sevilla in 2017).
I spent ten years at Nottingham Forest before transferring up the road.
I was born in Nottingham, but I switched my international allegiance from England's youth ranks to Jamaica in 2013 — and captained them to the 2015 Gold Cup final.
I captained Leicester City to the Premier League title in 2015–16 — and played every single minute of all 38 games.
footballers.story: The man who captained the greatest underdog story in Premier League history. Wes Morgan spent a decade in the Championship with Nottingham Forest, largely anonymous to the wider football world, before joining Leicester City and leading them to an almost incomprehensible 5000-1 title win in 2016. He played every single minute of that campaign — all 3,420 of them — an immovable wall at the back while Vardy and Mahrez grabbed the headlines. Not the quickest, not the most elegant, but utterly immense when it mattered. The fact that he's Jamaican-born, raised in Nottingham, and became the first Caribbean player to lift the Premier League trophy only adds to the fairy tale.
didYouKnow: He played every single minute of all 38 Premier League games during Leicester's miraculous 2015-16 title-winning season
He is the first Jamaican player to score in the Champions League, netting against Sevilla in 2017
He switched his international allegiance from England's youth ranks to Jamaica in 2013 and captained them to the 2015 Gold Cup final

footballers.name: Diego Godín
clues: I was born on the 16th of February 1986. I played 161 times for my country, scoring 8 goals.
I'm one of only six players ever to score in finals of both major European trophies — the Champions League and the Europa League.
I'm godfather to Antoine Griezmann's daughter — when France played us in the 2018 World Cup quarter-final, he didn't celebrate his goal out of respect.
I'm Uruguay's all-time most-capped player.
I scored a header against Barcelona on the final day of La Liga 2013–14 to win Atlético Madrid their first title in 18 years.
footballers.story: The warrior king of Atletico Madrid. Diego Godin was the heartbeat of Simeone's side for nearly a decade — the defender who threw his body in front of everything, who bled for the badge, and who headed in the goal against Barcelona on the final day of the 2013-14 season to clinch Atleti's first La Liga title in 18 years. Uruguay's most-capped player ever, he was the kind of centre-back who made strikers think twice about entering his penalty area. He came agonisingly close to Champions League glory twice, losing both finals to Real Madrid, but his legacy at the Calderon is untouchable.
didYouKnow: He is one of only six players ever to score in finals of both major European trophies — the Champions League and the Europa League
He is godfather to Antoine Griezmann's daughter — when France played Uruguay in the 2018 World Cup quarter-final, Griezmann did not celebrate his goal out of respect
He is Uruguay's all-time most-capped player with 161 appearances

footballers.name: Michail Antonio
clues: I was born on the 28th of March 1990. I have 5 goals from 24 appearances for my country — but not my country of birth.
I came through non-league football, starting out at Tooting & Mitcham before Reading signed me.
I once recreated the famous Dirty Dancing lift after scoring against Arsenal — using a cardboard cutout of myself.
I became my club's all-time Premier League top scorer in August 2021, replacing the previous holder who was himself an iconic Italian striker.
I won the Europa Conference League in 2023 — my club's first trophy in 43 years.
footballers.story: The ultimate non-league to Premier League success story. Michail Antonio started out at Tooting and Mitcham in the ninth tier of English football and fought his way up through Sheffield Wednesday, Nottingham Forest and a string of loans before West Ham took a punt on him. He repaid them by becoming the club's all-time Premier League top scorer, overtaking Paolo Di Canio, and winning the Europa Conference League in 2023. Built like a heavyweight boxer but with surprising technical ability, his cardboard-cutout celebration after scoring against Spurs became iconic. A serious car accident in December 2023 threatened everything, but his resilience — the same quality that got him out of non-league — defines him.
honours: West Ham all-time Premier League top scorer
didYouKnow: He started his career in the ninth tier of English football at Tooting and Mitcham United before working his way up to the Premier League
He once recreated the famous Dirty Dancing lift after scoring against Arsenal — using a life-size cardboard cutout of himself
He was born in London but chose to represent Jamaica internationally, qualifying through his parents' heritage

footballers.name: Dušan Tadić
clues: I was born on the 20th of November 1988. I played 111 times for my country, scoring 23 goals.
After my display in a 4–1 Champions League win at the Bernabéu in 2019, I became only the ninth player ever to receive a 10/10 rating from L'Équipe.
I captained my country at the 2022 World Cup.
I was the 2020–21 Dutch Footballer of the Year and Ajax Player of the Year.
After four years at Southampton I joined Ajax — and tore Real Madrid apart in the Champions League with a goal and two assists.
footballers.story: A player who looked decent at Southampton and then became genuinely world-class at Ajax. Dusan Tadic was a solid, creative presence during four years on the south coast, but few predicted what would happen when he moved to Amsterdam. Under Erik ten Hag he was transformed into one of the most complete attacking players in European football, orchestrating Ajax's sensational run to the 2019 Champions League semi-final — including that 4-1 demolition of Real Madrid at the Bernabeu, where L'Equipe gave him a perfect 10/10. Serbia's captain and most-capped outfield player, he proved that sometimes a player just needs the right system to unlock everything.
didYouKnow: He received a perfect 10/10 match rating from L'Equipe for his performance in Ajax's 4-1 Champions League victory over Real Madrid at the Bernabeu in 2019 — only the ninth player ever to receive the honour
He scored 105 goals and provided 112 assists across 241 matches during five seasons at Ajax
He captained Serbia at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar

footballers.name: Phil Jones
clues: I was born on the 21st of February 1992. I played 27 times for my country, never scoring a goal.
After a serious injury in 2019 I spent 20 months sidelined — described it as the lowest I've ever felt in my life.
I was once described by Sir Alex Ferguson in 2013: 'The way he is looking, he could be our best ever player.'
I won 4 major trophies at Manchester United including a Premier League and a Europa League.
I became a running joke for football's social media age — particularly for my contorted facial expressions.
footballers.story: Football's great 'what if.' Phil Jones arrived at Manchester United as a teenager so talented that Sir Alex Ferguson compared him to Duncan Edwards and predicted he could become the club's greatest ever player. The raw ability was undeniable — he could play centre-back, right-back, or midfield with equal conviction, and his whole-hearted defending was thrilling to watch. But injuries devastated him. A 20-month absence nearly broke him mentally, and by the end he was better known for meme-worthy facial expressions than for his football. He won four major trophies at Old Trafford and earned 27 England caps, but the overwhelming feeling is of a career that promised so much more.
didYouKnow: Sir Alex Ferguson said in 2013 that Phil Jones could become Manchester United's greatest ever player — a comparison to Duncan Edwards
He spent 20 months sidelined with a knee injury from 2019 to 2021, describing it as the lowest point of his life
His contorted facial expressions during tackles became one of football's most enduring memes, spawning countless social media compilations

footballers.name: Eidur Gudjohnsen
clues: I was born on the 15th of September 1978. I played 78 times for my country, scoring 26 goals.
In my first Premier League season I scored 19 goals, forming a strike partnership with Michael Ricketts.
I won back-to-back league titles in two different countries, lifting the trophy in England and then in Spain.
My father and I made history together — he was substituted off and I came on in the same international match in 1996.
I won the Champions League in 2009.
footballers.story: One of the most naturally gifted forwards to grace the Premier League, and somehow still underrated. Eidur Gudjohnsen scored 19 goals in his debut season at Bolton and went on to become a key part of Mourinho's title-winning Chelsea side, forming a lethal partnership with Hasselbaink and later playing alongside Drogba. Elegant, intelligent, and deceptively quick, he then moved to Barcelona — and won the Champions League in 2009. He made history with his father Arnor when the two appeared in the same international match for Iceland in 1996, the old man going off and the teenager coming on. A footballer who could play anywhere across the front line and did it all with class.
didYouKnow: He and his father Arnor made footballing history in April 1996 when Arnor was substituted off and Eidur came on as his replacement in the same international match for Iceland against Estonia
He won back-to-back league titles in two different countries — the Premier League with Chelsea in 2006 and La Liga with Barcelona in 2009
He scored 19 goals in his debut Premier League season at Bolton, forming a memorable partnership with Michael Ricketts

footballers.name: Pablo Hernández
clues: I was born on the 11th of April 1985. I played 4 times for my country, scoring 0 goals.
Before coming to the Premier League I played most of my career in La Liga.
I arrived in the Premier League after being signed by Michael Laudrup on loan from my Spanish club.
A manager who had previously coached in Argentina's top flight transformed my career in the Championship.
I won Player of the Season at this club when we got promoted.
footballers.story: El Mago. The magician of Elland Road. Pablo Hernandez arrived at Leeds on loan from Al-Arabi in 2018 as a 32-year-old with his best years supposedly behind him, and proceeded to become one of the most important players in the club's modern history. Under Marcelo Bielsa he was reborn — his vision, touch, and ability to unlock defences were perfectly suited to Bielsa's possession-based system. He scored crucial goals throughout the 2019-20 promotion campaign, none bigger than his winner at Swansea that clinched the Championship title and ended Leeds' 16-year exile from the top flight. Four caps for Spain barely scratch the surface of his talent.
honours: Leeds United Player of the Season (2019–20)
didYouKnow: His winner at Swansea in July 2020 clinched the Championship title and ended Leeds United's 16-year exile from the Premier League
He was nicknamed El Mago — the Magician — by Leeds fans for his ability to unlock defences with a single pass or piece of skill
He arrived at Leeds on loan from Qatari club Al-Arabi as a 32-year-old, yet became the most important creative player in their promotion campaign under Bielsa

footballers.name: Diego Simeone
clues: I was born on the 28th of April 1970. I played 106 times for my country, scoring 11 goals.
During my career I played alongside Diego Maradona.
I won the UEFA Cup with a Milan club and the domestic double with a Rome-based club.
I won a UEFA Cup and a league-and-cup double at the same Spanish club.
At the 1998 World Cup, I was involved in one of football's most infamous incidents.
footballers.story: Before he became the most intense manager in world football, Diego Simeone was one of the most intense midfielders. A snarling, combative presence who combined genuine quality with a win-at-all-costs mentality, he won trophies everywhere he went — the UEFA Cup and a league-cup double at Atletico Madrid, another UEFA Cup at Inter, and a Scudetto-Coppa double at Lazio. He is forever linked to the 1998 World Cup incident with David Beckham, whose red card after Simeone's theatrical fall became one of football's most controversial moments. Loved and loathed in equal measure, but never ignored.
didYouKnow: His theatrical reaction to David Beckham's kick at the 1998 World Cup led to Beckham's red card — one of the most infamous incidents in football history
He won major trophies as a player at three different clubs in three different countries — Atletico Madrid in Spain, Inter Milan and Lazio in Italy
He played alongside Diego Maradona at Sevilla during the 1992-93 season

footballers.name: Dominic Solanke
clues: I was born on the 14th of September 1997. I have been capped 5 times at senior level, scoring 0 goals. My first appearance came against Brazil in 2017.
I won the Golden Ball at the U-20 World Cup in South Korea, finishing as the tournament's best player.
I came through an academy in west London but never made a league appearance for the first team, leaving on a free transfer.
I scored just one Premier League goal in two seasons on Merseyside.
I scored 19 Championship goals to win promotion, before leaving for around £65 million.
footballers.story: A career that looked like it might stall completely before exploding into life on the south coast. Dominic Solanke left Chelsea's academy without a league appearance, moved to Liverpool, and managed just one Premier League goal in two seasons at Anfield. It looked grim. But Bournemouth backed him, and after a slow start he became one of the Championship's most lethal strikers, scoring 19 goals to fire them back to the Premier League. He followed that with 19 more in the top flight, earning a big-money move to Tottenham. The kid who won the Golden Ball at the 2017 U-20 World Cup had finally delivered on the promise — it just took longer than anyone expected.
honours: FIFA U-20 World Cup Golden Ball (2017)
didYouKnow: He won the Golden Ball as the best player at the 2017 FIFA U-20 World Cup in South Korea, finishing as England's top scorer in the tournament
He scored just one Premier League goal in two seasons at Liverpool before Bournemouth signed him for around 19 million pounds
His transfer to Tottenham in the summer of 2024 was worth around 65 million pounds, making him one of the most expensive English strikers ever

footballers.name: Gabriel Batistuta
clues: I was born on the 1st of February 1969. I played 78 times for my country, scoring 54 goals.
I started my career in Argentina at Newell's Old Boys and River Plate before moving to Europe at the age of 22.
I hold my country's record for the most hat-tricks at World Cup finals, and I scored in three consecutive tournaments.
I spent nine years at a club in Tuscany known for its purple shirts, becoming their all-time top scorer — and I cried when they were relegated.
I finally won the Scudetto when I moved to another Italian club in 2000.
footballers.story: Batigol. One of the purest strikers the game has ever seen. Gabriel Batistuta spent nine glorious years at Fiorentina, becoming their all-time top scorer and a deity in Florence — and when they were relegated in 1993 he stayed, which only deepened the love. His left foot was a weapon of mass destruction: free-kicks, volleys, headers, he scored them all with ferocious power and precision. He famously cried when Fiorentina finally went down, then moved to Roma in 2000 and immediately won the Scudetto. Argentina's record World Cup hat-trick scorer, he is the kind of striker they simply do not make any more.
didYouKnow: He stayed at Fiorentina when they were relegated to Serie B in 1993, cementing his legendary status in Florence — fans still consider him the greatest player in the club's history
He holds Argentina's record for the most hat-tricks at World Cup finals and scored in three consecutive tournaments (1994, 1998, 2002)
He suffered so badly from ankle injuries in later life that he once asked doctors to amputate his feet to end the chronic pain

footballers.name: Adel Taarabt
clues: I was born on the 24th of May 1990. I have 4 goals from 30 appearances for my country — but not my country of birth.
I joined a north London club's academy as a teenager but managed just two league appearances for their first team.
A manager once said the only way he could get any weight off me was when I had tonsillitis.
I won the Championship, finishing as the league's Player of the Season.
I won the league title in Portugal with one of the country's biggest clubs.
footballers.story: A mercurial talent who could dribble past entire defences on his day but rarely produced it consistently enough to satisfy his managers. Taarabt arrived at Tottenham as a teenager from Lens but never broke through, finding his feet instead at QPR where he was the Championship's standout performer in 2010–11. His close control and audacious skill made him a fans' favourite, but his attitude was questioned at every stop. A loan to AC Milan offered a glimpse of what might have been, before he settled at Benfica where he finally found stability, winning multiple league titles and becoming a reliable squad player into his thirties.
didYouKnow: He once nutmegged three players in a single run during a Championship match against Bristol City
He represented both France and Morocco at youth level before committing to Morocco
Harry Redknapp called him the best player in the Championship by a mile during QPR's promotion season

footballers.name: Antonio Di Natale
clues: I was born on the 13th of October 1977. I scored 11 goals in 42 appearances for my country.
I spent almost my entire career in Italian football.
I didn't score my first Serie A goal until I was 24, and didn't become a regular starter until I was nearly 30.
I won the Serie A top scorer award twice in consecutive seasons, despite playing for a club that never challenged for the title.
I turned down a move to one of Italy's biggest clubs because my family had fallen in love with life in Friuli.
footballers.story: A one-club legend in Serie A's truest sense. Di Natale spent 12 years at Udinese, becoming the club's all-time top scorer and one of the most prolific strikers in Italian football history. Small in stature but explosive in the box, he hit his peak remarkably late — scoring 29 league goals at the age of 33 in 2010–11, finishing as Serie A's top scorer for two consecutive seasons. He turned down moves to Juventus and other giants out of loyalty to Udinese, retiring as one of the most beloved figures in Italian football.
didYouKnow: He scored 227 goals for Udinese, a record that may never be broken
He turned down a move to Juventus in 2011 when he was the top scorer in Serie A
He scored against every single Serie A team during his career

footballers.name: Papiss Cissé
clues: I was born on the 3rd of June 1985. I scored 16 goals in approximately 30 appearances for my country.
I scored 13 goals in my first 12 Premier League appearances after joining in January.
I wore the number 9 shirt at my English club — previously worn by the club's all-time record scorer.
In the Bundesliga, I scored 22 goals in a single season — a record for an African player at the time.
I scored a goal from 37 yards with the outside of my foot against Chelsea that won BBC Goal of the Season.
footballers.story: Cissé arrived at Newcastle in January 2012 as a relatively unknown quantity from Freiburg — and promptly scored 13 goals in 14 games to finish the season, including two of the most spectacular strikes the Premier League has ever seen, both in the same match against Chelsea. His curling, physics-defying effort from outside the box at Stamford Bridge is still regularly voted one of the greatest goals in league history. Though injuries and inconsistency followed, that electric first half-season cemented him as a Tyneside legend.
honours: Newcastle United Player of the Year nominee (2012)
didYouKnow: His goal against Chelsea at Stamford Bridge in 2012 won the Premier League Goal of the Season
He scored 13 goals in his first 14 Premier League appearances — one of the best debut half-seasons ever
He initially wore the number 9 shirt at Newcastle, a shirt with enormous pressure and history at the club

footballers.name: Fernandinho
clues: I was born on the 4th of May 1985. I scored 2 goals in 53 appearances for my country.
I started my country's most humiliating World Cup defeat on home soil — and was substituted at half-time.
I've played as a defensive midfielder, central midfielder, and centre-back at the highest level.
I spent eight years in the Ukrainian Premier League before moving to England.
I won five Premier League titles with the same club.
footballers.story: The quiet enforcer behind Manchester City's dominance. Fernandinho arrived from Shakhtar Donetsk in 2013 and spent a decade at the Etihad, winning five Premier League titles and becoming one of the most reliable midfielders in the league's history. Never the headline act alongside the likes of Silva, De Bruyne and Agüero, he was the player Guardiola trusted most to control the tempo and do the dirty work. He captained the side in his final seasons, a testament to his leadership and professionalism.
didYouKnow: He won more League Cups (6) than any other competition during his career
He played over 350 games for Manchester City across 9 seasons
He was named Manchester City captain for the 2020–21 season at the age of 35

footballers.name: Brad Friedel
clues: I was born on the 18th of May 1971. I played 82 times for my country, scoring 0 goals.
I signed for a club on Merseyside but struggled to break into the first team, making only a handful of appearances.
I once scored from a corner kick in the 90th minute — one of only a handful of goalkeepers to score in the Premier League.
A manager who first worked with me in Turkey later signed me for an English club, where I became first choice for nearly a decade.
I hold the all-time record for the most consecutive Premier League appearances — over 300 in a row.
footballers.story: The most successful American goalkeeper in Premier League history. Friedel set a then-record of 310 consecutive Premier League appearances between 2004 and 2012, playing for Blackburn, Aston Villa and Tottenham without missing a single league game for over eight years. A commanding presence in goal with outstanding shot-stopping ability, he was a model of consistency in an era when American players in the English top flight were a rarity. He also famously scored a goal for Blackburn in the League Cup.
honours: 310 consecutive Premier League appearances (record at the time)
footballers.numbers: 0 goals in 82 caps for United States
didYouKnow: He played in three World Cups for the United States (1998, 2002, 2006)
He scored a last-minute equaliser for Blackburn against Charlton in the 2004 League Cup
He was still playing Premier League football at the age of 42

footballers.name: Andriy Shevchenko
clues: I was born on the 29th of September 1976. I played 111 times for my country, scoring 48 goals.
I won the Ballon d’Or after years of prolific goalscoring at the highest level.
I won five league titles in eastern Europe before moving to Italy, where I added a Champions League to my collection.
I won the 2003 Champions League final, scoring the winning penalty in a shootout against Juventus at Old Trafford.
I moved to England for big money but the transfer proved unsuccessful — I never came close to matching my prolific form from Italy.
footballers.story: One of the deadliest strikers of his generation. Shevchenko burst onto the scene at Dynamo Kyiv before becoming the focal point of a fearsome AC Milan attack for eight glorious seasons. His pace, movement and clinical finishing brought him the Ballon d'Or in 2004 and over 170 goals for the Rossoneri. The move to Chelsea for £30 million never worked — he looked a shadow of his former self in England — but his legacy in Italian football is untouchable. He later managed Ukraine at Euro 2020, guiding them to the quarter-finals.
didYouKnow: He scored the winning penalty in the 2003 Champions League final against Juventus
He is AC Milan's second all-time top scorer with 175 goals
He grew up in Kyiv and was five years old when the Chernobyl disaster occurred less than 100km away

footballers.name: Luke Shaw
clues: I was born on the 12th of July 1995. I have over 30 caps for my country, scoring 3 goals.
I moved for a fee of around £30 million as a teenager, making me the most expensive teenager in world football at the time.
I had a well-documented difficult relationship with José Mourinho, who publicly criticised my fitness and commitment multiple times.
I came through the academy at Southampton before moving to one of the biggest clubs in England at the age of 18.
I started for England throughout Euro 2020 (played in 2021), including the final at Wembley where my country lost on penalties to Italy.
footballers.story: Shaw burst onto the scene at Southampton as one of the most exciting young full-backs in English football, earning a £30 million move to Manchester United at just 18 — making him the most expensive teenager in world football at the time. A horrific double leg break against PSV in 2015 threatened his career, but he fought back. His peak came at Euro 2020, where he scored the opening goal against Italy in the final within two minutes. His relationship with Mourinho became one of football's most public player-manager feuds, but Shaw consistently proved his quality when fit and confident.
didYouKnow: He was the youngest player to appear for Southampton in the Premier League, debuting at 16
His goal against Italy at Euro 2020 was the fastest ever scored in a European Championship final
He was publicly criticised by José Mourinho on multiple occasions, including being told he only played well when Mourinho picked the team for him

footballers.name: Antonio Cassano
clues: I was born on the 12th of July 1982. I played 39 times for my country, scoring 10 goals.
I’m one of very few players to have played for both Milan clubs — starting at one and crossing the city to the other.

I was as famous for my waistline as my talent — I once reportedly gained 14 kilos between seasons. Managers struggled to control me, and my fallouts with coaches became as legendary as my ability on the ball.
I won La Liga with Real Madrid and the Serie A title with AC Milan.
My former coach coined a word after me — “cassanata” — which Italian journalists still use today as a byword for any behaviour incompatible with team spirit.
footballers.story: Arguably the most naturally gifted Italian footballer of his generation — and almost certainly the most frustrating. Cassano emerged as a teenage sensation at Bari, earning a move to Roma where his outrageous skill thrilled the Stadio Olimpico. But everywhere he went, controversy followed: weight problems, arguments with coaches, and a self-destructive streak that prevented him from fulfilling his extraordinary potential. He won titles at Real Madrid and AC Milan but was never a regular at either. A stroke at 29 nearly ended his life, yet he returned to play on for several more years. His talent was never in doubt — only his temperament.
didYouKnow: His former coach Fabio Capello coined the term "cassanata" to describe behaviour incompatible with team spirit — the word is still used by Italian journalists
He reportedly gained 14 kilos between seasons on more than one occasion
He suffered a stroke during a flight with AC Milan in 2011 but made a full recovery and returned to football within months

footballers.name: David Trezeguet
clues: I was born on the 15th of October 1977. I played 71 times for my country, scoring 34 goals.
I formed a striking partnership with Thierry Henry at my first European club, winning two league titles.
I won both a World Cup and a European Championship with my country within two years.
I scored the golden goal in extra time of a major international final — beating Italy with a volley into the roof of the net.
When my club was relegated after a match-fixing scandal, I was one of the few stars who stayed to help them return.
footballers.story: Born in France to Argentine parents, Trezeguet was one of the most lethal finishers in European football during the early 2000s. He scored the golden goal that won France Euro 2000 and went on to become Juventus's all-time top foreign scorer, netting over 170 goals in a decade in Turin. A pure poacher with razor-sharp instincts in the box, he remained loyal to Juventus even after their relegation to Serie B in 2006, helping them win promotion back to the top flight. His missed penalty in the 2006 World Cup final against Italy remains one of football's most heartbreaking moments.
didYouKnow: He scored the golden goal in the Euro 2000 final against Italy — a left-footed volley in extra time
He is Juventus's all-time top foreign scorer with 171 goals
He stayed with Juventus in Serie B after the Calciopoli scandal when most stars left, earning him legendary status among Juve fans

footballers.name: Danny Rose
clues: I was born on the 2nd of July 1990. I played 29 times for my country, scoring 0 goals.
I started a Champions League final — but finished on the losing side.
I was converted from a winger to a full-back, a decision that led to me playing in a World Cup semi-final.
I came through an academy in Yorkshire before moving to London as a teenager.
On my league debut, I scored a long-range volley in a derby match that won Goal of the Season.
footballers.story: Rose announced himself to the world with a thunderous 30-yard volley on his Tottenham debut against Arsenal in 2010 — a goal that won Match of the Day's Goal of the Season. After loan spells at various clubs, he established himself as Spurs' first-choice left-back under Pochettino and earned regular England caps. He was part of the Tottenham side that reached the 2019 Champions League final. Rose was also one of the first high-profile players to speak publicly about his struggles with depression, helping to break the stigma around mental health in football.
didYouKnow: His debut goal against Arsenal — a dipping volley from nearly 30 yards — won the 2009–10 Goal of the Season
He spoke openly about suffering from depression in 2019, becoming one of the first active Premier League players to do so
He was racially abused while playing for England in Serbia in 2012, an incident that led to sanctions against the Serbian FA

footballers.name: Roberto Soldado
clues: I was born on the 27th of May 1985. I played 12 times for my country, scoring 7 goals.
I came through the youth academy at Real Madrid but barely featured for the first team — I was sent out on loan before finding my feet elsewhere in Spain.
I scored only 7 Premier League goals across two seasons — one of the most expensive attacking flops of the 2010s.
After my struggles in England, I returned to Spain and rebuilt my career at Villarreal — a club known for its famous yellow shirts.
My move to the Premier League was meant to help replace the goals of a departing Welsh superstar, but I could never fill those boots.
footballers.story: One of the most clinical finishers in La Liga history during his Valencia years. Soldado scored over 80 league goals for Los Che, peaking with 30 in all competitions in 2012–13 — earning a £26 million move to Tottenham as part of the spending spree that followed Gareth Bale's departure. In England, the goals dried up spectacularly: just 7 in two Premier League seasons. A return to Villarreal revived his career in Spain, and he later had spells at Fenerbahçe and Granada before retiring — respected in La Liga, but remembered in England as one of the most expensive flops of the decade.
didYouKnow: He scored over 80 league goals for Valencia, peaking with 24 in the 2012-13 season, but managed only 7 Premier League goals across two seasons at Tottenham
He was signed as part of the spending spree following Gareth Bale's departure from Tottenham, costing around 26 million pounds
He returned to Spain with Villarreal and rebuilt his reputation, scoring consistently for three seasons before winding down his career at Granada and Levante

footballers.name: Emile Heskey
clues: I was born on the 11th of January 1978. I played 62 times for my country, scoring 7 goals.
I made my England debut against Hungary in April 1999 and went on to become a regular in the squad for the next five years.
Moved to Liverpool for £11 million in 2000, winning a cup treble in my first season.
Had spells at Birmingham City, Wigan Athletic and Aston Villa after leaving Anfield.
Known for my physical strength and unselfish hold-up play, I was a favourite strike partner for Michael Owen.
footballers.story: A powerful, unselfish striker whose contribution was always greater than his goal tally suggested. Heskey burst onto the scene at Leicester City before an £11 million move to Liverpool in 2000, where he won a treble of cups in his first season. His hold-up play and physical presence made him the ideal foil for Michael Owen at club level and later Wayne Rooney for England. Often mocked by a generation of internet fans, those who played alongside him consistently rated him as one of the best strike partners in English football. Later spells at Birmingham, Wigan and Aston Villa brought diminishing returns, but his place in Premier League history is secure.
didYouKnow: He won a treble of cups in his first season at Liverpool — the FA Cup, League Cup and UEFA Cup — all in 2001
Michael Owen, Wayne Rooney and other strikers consistently named him as their favourite strike partner, praising his selfless hold-up play
He finished his career in Australia with Newcastle Jets in the A-League before a brief return to English football with Bolton Wanderers

footballers.name: Lukas Podolski
clues: I was born on the 4th of June 1985. I played 130 times for my country, scoring 49 goals — the third-highest tally in their history.
I won the Best Young Player award at the 2006 World Cup, hosted in my adopted home country.
I spent three years at Bayern Munich but was never a regular starter, often playing second fiddle to the likes of Ribéry and Robben.
I spent three seasons in the Premier League, known for my powerful left-footed shooting but less so for my work rate off the ball.

I scored a thunderbolt volley against Montpellier in the Champions League at the Emirates and later netted a stunning strike against Bayern Munich at the same ground — my left foot was my greatest weapon.
footballers.story: Lukas Podolski burst onto the world stage as an 18-year-old at Euro 2004 and never looked back. A left foot that could crack concrete, the German-born Pole became one of the Nationalmannschaft's most beloved figures across nearly two decades of international service. At club level he shone brightest at his hometown Köln, though spells at Bayern Munich, Arsenal, and Inter Milan showed his quality across Europe's biggest stages. His infectious smile and thunderous strikes made him a fan favourite everywhere he played, and his 130 caps placed him among Germany's most-capped players of all time.
didYouKnow: He won the Best Young Player award at the 2006 World Cup hosted in Germany, finishing as the tournament's joint third-highest scorer
He is Germany's third-highest all-time goalscorer with 49 goals in 130 caps, behind only Miroslav Klose and Gerd Muller
He was born in Poland and moved to Germany aged two — he holds both Polish and German citizenship

footballers.name: Morten Gamst Pedersen
clues: I was born on the 8th of September 1981. I played 83 times for my country, scoring 17 goals.
I have Sami heritage and once fronted a Norwegian footballer boyband called The Players.
My Euro 2008 qualifier goal against Hungary was so reminiscent of van Basten's 1988 final volley that the Norwegian media nicknamed me 'van Gamsten'.
I was at the centre of one of the Premier League's strangest scuffles when Mikel Arteta poked me in the eye after a tackle in 2010.
I spent nine years at Blackburn Rovers — famous for my left foot and dead-ball deliveries.
footballers.story: Blackburn's Norwegian left foot — nine years at Ewood Park, hundreds of inswinging deliveries, and a knack for the spectacular. Never the quickest, never the flashiest, but you knew exactly what you were getting: dead-ball danger and a left peg straight from the pages of an old coaching manual.
footballers.numbers: 118 goals in 633 club appearances · 17 in 83 for Norway
didYouKnow: His 2006 Euro qualifier goal against Hungary was so reminiscent of van Basten's 1988 final volley that the Norwegian media nicknamed him "van Gamsten".
Was at the centre of one of the Premier League's odder scuffles when Mikel Arteta poked him in the cheek and then the eye after a tackle in 2010.
Has Sami heritage and fronted a Norwegian footballer boyband called "The Players" — released a single for an anti-crime charity campaign.

footballers.name: Mario Götze
clues: I was born on the 3rd of June 1992. I played 66 times for my country, scoring 17 goals.
I was the youngest goalscorer in my club's Bundesliga history when I scored on my debut aged 17.
I became the youngest player to score in the Bundesliga for my club, and by 19 I was considered one of the most exciting young players in world football.
I scored the winning goal in a World Cup final — a chest-and-volley finish in extra time against Argentina at the Maracanã.
I won the Bundesliga and the DFB-Pokal in my first season at Bayern Munich.
footballers.story: The boy who won the World Cup. At 22, Götze controlled André Schürrle's cross on his chest and volleyed past the Argentine goalkeeper in extra time of the 2014 final at the Maracanã — a goal that defined a generation. He had already been one of Europe's most exciting teenagers at Borussia Dortmund before a controversial move to Bayern Munich in 2013. But a metabolic disorder disrupted his career, robbing him of his explosive pace and threatening his future in the game. He returned to Dortmund but was never quite the same, eventually finding a late renaissance at Eintracht Frankfurt before retiring in 2024.
honours: Bundesliga x5 (Borussia Dortmund x2, Bayern Munich x3)
DFB-Pokal x3 (Borussia Dortmund x1, Bayern Munich x2)
didYouKnow: He scored the winning goal in the 2014 World Cup final with a chest-and-volley finish in extra time against Argentina at the Maracana
He was diagnosed with a metabolic disorder (myopathy) in 2017 that caused significant weight gain and robbed him of his explosive pace
He was the youngest goalscorer in Borussia Dortmund's Bundesliga history when he scored on his debut aged 17

footballers.name: Eric Abidal
clues: I was born on the 11th of September 1979. I played 67 times for my country, scoring 0 goals.
I won three consecutive league titles with a club in central-eastern France before moving to Spain.
I won four league titles and two Champions League medals with the same Spanish club.
I could play both left-back and centre-back at the highest level.
I later became sporting director at the club where I'd won the Champions League.
footballers.story: Eric Abidal's football story is one of extraordinary courage both on and off the pitch. The French left-back built his reputation at Lyon during their dominant run of seven consecutive Ligue 1 titles before earning a move to Barcelona in 2007. At Camp Nou he became a key figure in Pep Guardiola's all-conquering side, winning the Champions League in 2009. Then came his greatest battle — diagnosed with a liver tumour in 2011, he underwent surgery, returned to football, and lifted the Champions League trophy again in 2011 as captain, in one of sport's most emotional moments.
didYouKnow: He was diagnosed with a liver tumour in 2011, underwent surgery, and returned to lift the Champions League trophy as captain just two months later
He required a liver transplant in 2012, with his cousin donating part of his liver, and still returned to play professional football
He later became sporting director at Barcelona, the club where he had won two Champions League titles as a player

footballers.name: Salomón Rondón
clues: I was born on the 16th of September 1989. I played over 100 times for my country, scoring more than 40 goals.
I am my country's all-time leading goalscorer — but I have never played at a World Cup, because my country has never qualified.
I captained my country in the Copa América on multiple occasions.
I had a brief spell at Everton under Rafa Benítez, but I struggled to make an impact.
I spent three seasons at West Bromwich Albion, becoming a firm fan favourite for my work rate and aerial ability.
footballers.story: Venezuela's greatest ever footballer and a throwback to the classic number nine. Rondón is his country's all-time leading goalscorer despite never playing at a World Cup — a consequence of Venezuela's struggles to qualify rather than any lack of ability. He made his name in European football with West Brom, where three seasons of hard-running, aerial dominance and spectacular goals made him a firm fan favourite. A loan season at Newcastle under Rafa Benítez was equally successful, before spells in China and a brief stint at Everton. He remains a national hero in a country where football has always lived in baseball's shadow.
didYouKnow: He is Venezuela's all-time leading goalscorer with over 40 goals but has never played at a World Cup because Venezuela has never qualified
He spent three seasons at West Brom where he became a cult hero, followed by a successful loan at Newcastle under Rafa Benitez
He won the Argentine league title with River Plate in 2023 and the CONCACAF Champions Cup with Pachuca in 2024

footballers.name: Yakubu Aiyegbeni
clues: I was born on the 22nd of November 1982. I played 58 times for my country, scoring 21 goals.
I scored seven goals in eight Champions League matches for an Israeli club — including a hat-trick against Olympiakos.
David Moyes signed me for a club-record fee in 2007 — I scored 21 goals in all competitions in my first season.
I scored all four goals in a 4–2 Premier League win in December 2011.
I scored 25 Premier League goals in 82 appearances for a club on Merseyside.
footballers.story: The Yak. Built like a wardrobe, finished like a fox. Quietly one of the Premier League's most efficient strikers of his era — Portsmouth, Boro, Everton — racking up goals while half the league forgot how good he actually was.
footballers.numbers: 170+ league goals across his career · 21 in 58 for Nigeria
didYouKnow: Scored seven in eight Champions League games for Maccabi Haifa in 2002–03 — including a hat-trick against Olympiakos — which earned him his Portsmouth move.
Scored all four in Blackburn's 4–2 win over Swansea in December 2011 — his first four-goal haul since the Pompey days.
Played barefoot on the streets of Benin City until his brother bought him his first pair of boots aged 12.

footballers.name: Victor Moses
clues: I was born on the 12th of December 1990. I played 38 times for my country, scoring 12 goals.
I won the Africa Cup of Nations in 2013, setting up two goals in the semi-final against Mali.
I scored on my league debut for Liverpool and also won a penalty in an FA Cup semi-final for Chelsea against Tottenham.
I lost both of my parents in religious riots in Nigeria when I was 11 years old and was brought to England as an asylum seeker.
I was reinvented as a right wing-back by an Italian manager — and it transformed both my career and my team's season.
footballers.story: A remarkable story of resilience and reinvention. Moses lost both parents in religious violence in Nigeria aged 11 and arrived in England as an asylum seeker. He worked his way through Crystal Palace and Wigan Athletic before Chelsea signed him in 2012 — but years of loans to Liverpool, Stoke and West Ham followed, and his career seemed destined for mid-table obscurity. Then Antonio Conte arrived and transformed Moses into a right wing-back in his title-winning 3-4-3 system. Moses played virtually every game as Chelsea won the 2016–17 Premier League — one of the most unlikely reinventions in the league's history.
didYouKnow: He lost both his parents in religious violence in Nigeria aged 11 and came to England as an asylum seeker
Antonio Conte reinvented him as a right wing-back in his 3-4-3 system, and Moses played virtually every game as Chelsea won the 2016-17 Premier League
He won the Africa Cup of Nations with Nigeria in 2013, setting up two goals in the semi-final against Mali

footballers.name: Chris Sutton
clues: I was born on the 10th of March 1973. I played once for my country, scoring 0 goals.
Signed for a club record £5 million fee, making me one of the most expensive British players at the time.
Scored in a famous 4–3 Champions League win over Juventus at Celtic Park in 2001.
Won the Premier League title in 1994–95 as part of the famous SAS strike partnership.
Now a well-known football pundit, regularly appearing on BBC Radio 5 Live and Match of the Day.
footballers.story: Half of the most famous strike partnership of the 1990s. Sutton formed the SAS — Sutton and Shearer — at Blackburn Rovers, firing them to the 1994–95 Premier League title with 15 goals. A big-money move to Chelsea promised more glory, but he was played out of position and never settled. It was at Celtic where Sutton truly flourished again, scoring 86 goals in five seasons and forming another prolific partnership with Henrik Larsson. His headed goal in the famous 3–2 Champions League win against Juventus at Celtic Park in 2001 remains one of the great European nights. Now better known as a forthright pundit on BBC Radio 5 Live.
didYouKnow: He formed the famous SAS strike partnership with Alan Shearer at Blackburn, scoring 15 league goals as they won the 1994-95 Premier League title
He scored in Celtic's famous 4-3 Champions League win over Juventus at Celtic Park in 2001
He won just one England cap despite his prolific goalscoring record — a 12-minute substitute appearance against Cameroon in 1997

footballers.name: Hatem Ben Arfa
clues: I was born on the 7th of March 1987. I played 15 times for my country, scoring 2 goals.
I started my career at Lyon and then Marseille, crossing one of French football's fiercest rivalries.
I scored one of the greatest solo goals in Premier League history — dribbling past four players before finishing — and the crowd erupted.
I fell out with managers at almost every club I played for — my talent was never in doubt, but my attitude was a constant source of controversy.
At Newcastle, I became a cult hero despite playing fewer than 80 league games — my highlights reel was worth the admission alone.
footballers.story: The most talented footballer to never fulfil his potential — or so the story goes. Ben Arfa emerged from Lyon's academy alongside Benzema and was considered by many to be the more gifted of the two. Spells at Lyon, Marseille and Newcastle showcased dribbling ability that could embarrass any defender, including a solo goal against Bolton that is regularly voted one of the Premier League's greatest. But fallings-out with coaches limited him at every turn. A brief renaissance at Nice — 18 goals in 2015–16 — earned a PSG contract, but he barely played a game. The talent was never in question; the temperament always was.
honours: Coupe de France x3 (Lyon, PSG, Rennes)
didYouKnow: He emerged from Lyon's academy alongside Karim Benzema and was considered by many to be the more naturally gifted of the two
His solo goal against Bolton for Newcastle — dribbling past four players — is regularly voted one of the greatest Premier League goals ever scored
He scored 18 goals for Nice in 2015-16, earning a contract at PSG, but barely played a game during his time in Paris

footballers.name: Gervinho
clues: I was born on the 27th of May 1987. I played 86 times for my country, scoring 23 goals.
I won the Ligue 1 title with Lille in 2011, helping them beat PSG and Marseille to a shock championship.
I was sent off on my Premier League debut after slapping an opponent, setting the tone for a frustrating time in England.
After a difficult spell in the Premier League, I moved to Italy where I completely reinvented myself as one of Serie A's most exciting forwards.
I spent two prolific seasons at Roma, where my pace and directness made me a completely different player to the one Arsenal fans remembered.
footballers.story: A career of two wildly different halves. At Lille, Gervinho was electric — his pace and direct running helped deliver a shock Ligue 1 title in 2011, beating PSG and Marseille to the championship. Arsenal came calling, but from a red card on his Premier League debut for slapping Joey Barton against Newcastle, his time in England was defined by missed chances and frustration. The open goals he failed to convert became internet folklore. Then came Roma, and a complete reinvention — in Serie A, his speed and finishing suddenly clicked, and he became one of Italy's most exciting forwards for two prolific seasons before moving to Chinese football.
footballers.numbers: 23 goals in 86 caps for Ivory Coast
didYouKnow: He was sent off on his Premier League debut for slapping Joey Barton during a match against Newcastle
After a frustrating spell at Arsenal, he completely reinvented himself at Roma, becoming one of Serie A's most exciting forwards for two seasons
He won the Africa Cup of Nations with Ivory Coast in 2015 and the Turkish Super Lig with Trabzonspor in 2022

footballers.name: Radamel Falcao
clues: I was born on the 10th of February 1986. I played 104 times for my country, scoring 36 goals — making me their all-time record scorer.
I won the Europa League twice in three years with two different clubs — Porto and then Atlético Madrid.
I moved to Monaco for around €60 million in 2013, making me one of the most expensive transfers in history at the time.
I was loaned to both Manchester United and Chelsea in consecutive seasons — scoring a combined total of just 5 league goals.
I was nicknamed 'El Tigre' — and at my peak, I was considered one of the three best strikers in the world.
footballers.story: El Tigre — one of the most devastating strikers of his generation. Falcao was almost unstoppable at his peak, scoring 17 goals in a single Europa League campaign for Porto before terrorising La Liga with Atlético Madrid. His hat-trick in the 2012 Copa del Rey final against Real Madrid at the Bernabéu was the performance that defined him. A €60 million move to Monaco followed, but a catastrophic knee injury before the 2014 World Cup changed everything. Loan spells at Manchester United and Chelsea were disasters, yielding just five league goals combined. Colombia's all-time record scorer deserved a better ending to his European adventure.
didYouKnow: He scored a hat-trick in the 2012 Copa del Rey final against Real Madrid at the Bernabeu — one of the greatest individual performances in a Spanish cup final
A catastrophic ACL injury before the 2014 World Cup derailed his career — loan spells at Manchester United and Chelsea yielded just 5 league goals combined
He is Colombia's all-time record goalscorer with 36 goals in 104 appearances

footballers.name: Sylvain Distin
clues: I was born on the 16th of December 1977. I never played for my country, never scoring a goal.
I was approached to play for Guadeloupe at the 2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup but turned it down to focus on Everton's preseason.
I was on the pitch when my goalkeeper scored from inside his own half against Bolton in January 2012 — one of the most remarkable goals in Premier League history.
I made over 450 Premier League appearances — more than any other foreign outfielder in the competition's history.
I famously announced my 'international retirement' on Twitter in 2014 — having never won a single France cap.
footballers.story: The Premier League's most-capped foreign outfielder — and yet not a single France cap to show for it. Manchester City, Portsmouth, Everton — calm, dominant in the air, the kind of defender every relegation-threatened side wished they had. The best French centre-half never to play for France.
footballers.numbers: 402+ Premier League appearances (most by any foreign outfielder) · 0 France caps
didYouKnow: Despite playing more Premier League games than any other foreign outfielder, he was never picked by France — announced his "international retirement" on Twitter in 2014, deadpan.
Was approached to represent Guadeloupe at the 2011 CONCACAF Gold Cup but turned it down to focus on Everton's preseason.
Assisted Tim Howard's freak 100-yard goal in 2011–12 — only the fourth keeper-from-open-play goal in Premier League history.

footballers.name: Nigel Winterburn
clues: I was born on the 11th of December 1963. I played 2 times for my country, scoring 0 goals.
Won three league titles and two FA Cups during a 13-year spell in English football.
Signed from Wimbledon in 1987 by George Graham — I went on to win both the old First Division and the Premier League with the same club.
I finished my career at West Ham United, playing until the age of 39 — one of the oldest outfield players in the Premier League at the time.
I was part of one of the most elite defences in Premier League history, alongside Adams, Bould and Dixon.
footballers.story: A cornerstone of one of English football's greatest ever defences. Winterburn spent 13 years at Arsenal, forming a back four alongside Adams, Bould and Dixon that became the gold standard of the early Premier League era. Signed from Wimbledon by George Graham in 1987, he won three league titles — including the dramatic 1988–89 Championship decider at Anfield — two FA Cups and a European Cup Winners' Cup. Reliable rather than flashy, his positioning and tackling made him one of the most consistent left-backs of his generation. He finished his career at West Ham, playing until the age of 38.
didYouKnow: He was part of the legendary Arsenal back four alongside Tony Adams, Steve Bould and Lee Dixon — one of the greatest defensive units in English football history
He was on the pitch for the dramatic last-minute title win at Anfield in 1989, when Michael Thomas scored to clinch the championship
He played until the age of 39 at West Ham, making him one of the oldest outfield players in Premier League history at the time

footballers.name: Ramires
clues: I was born on the 24th of March 1987. I played 52 times for my country, scoring 4 goals.
I won every major honour available in English football — the Premier League, FA Cup, League Cup, Champions League, and Europa League — all at the same club.
I spent one season in Portugal, helping my club win the league title — performing so well that an English club paid €22 million for me.

On the stroke of half-time at the Camp Nou, with my team 2–0 down in a Champions League semi-final, I produced a delicate chip over the goalkeeper that became the defining moment of our European campaign — and reportedly caused the creator of this app to storm out of the pub in disgust at Barcelona, only to be coaxed back in.
I made 251 appearances across six seasons at Chelsea, winning a trophy in almost every year I was there.
footballers.story: The tireless Brazilian box-to-box midfielder who produced one of the most iconic moments in Champions League history. Ramires arrived at Chelsea from Benfica in 2010 and spent six seasons at Stamford Bridge, winning every major honour available in English football — the Premier League, FA Cup, League Cup, Champions League, and Europa League. His defining moment came at the Camp Nou in the 2012 Champions League semi-final: with Chelsea 2-0 down on the night, he collected the ball on the halfway line, drove forward, and produced a delicate chip over Victor Valdes on the stroke of half-time that changed the entire complexion of the tie. Chelsea went on to win the tournament. He was all energy and lung capacity, covering every blade of grass, though his decision-making could frustrate. A big-money move to Chinese football in 2016 effectively ended his European story, but that chip will live forever.
didYouKnow: His delicate chip over Victor Valdes at the Camp Nou in the 2012 Champions League semi-final is considered one of the most important goals in Chelsea's history
He won every major honour available in English football during his six years at Chelsea — the Premier League, FA Cup, League Cup, Champions League, and Europa League
He spent just one season at Benfica before Chelsea paid around 22 million euros for him — he had helped Benfica win the Portuguese league title

footballers.name: Fernando Hierro
clues: I was born on the 23rd of March 1968. I played 89 times for my country, scoring 29 goals.
I captained my country across four World Cups and two European Championships.
I ended my playing career in the Premier League, joining on the advice of a former teammate.
I won five league titles and three Champions League trophies during 14 years at my main club.
I scored 127 goals in 601 appearances for one of the world’s greatest clubs, often from free-kicks and penalties.
footballers.story: El Mariscal — The Marshal — a centre-back who scored 127 goals in 601 appearances for Real Madrid, a record that defies belief for a defender. Hierro was the rock at the heart of Madrid’s defence for 14 years, winning five La Liga titles and three Champions League trophies while also captaining Spain across four World Cups. His free-kicks and penalties were as lethal as most strikers’ finishing. A bitter departure from the Bernabeu in 2003 led to a brief stint at Al-Rayyan in Qatar before a single season at Bolton Wanderers, where Sam Allardyce convinced him to try English football. He later managed Spain at the 2018 World Cup as an emergency appointment — but his legacy is as one of the greatest defenders in the history of the game.
didYouKnow: He scored 127 goals in 601 appearances for Real Madrid — an astonishing record for a centre-back that included free-kicks, penalties, and headers
He captained Spain across four World Cups and two European Championships, earning 89 caps and scoring 29 goals
He was appointed Spain manager just two days before the 2018 World Cup after Julen Lopetegui was sacked for agreeing to join Real Madrid

footballers.name: Rafael Márquez
clues: I played in five World Cups (2002, 2006, 2010, 2014 and 2018), scoring 3 goals across 19 games.
I am currently an assistant manager at this World Cup, for the country I played for.
I'm the only player in history to captain my country at five different World Cup finals. Guinness gave me the record for it.
I won four league titles and two Champions Leagues in seven seasons at one of the biggest clubs in Spain.
I'm Mexican. They called me 'El Káiser' at the Camp Nou.
footballers.story: Rafael Márquez was the calm at the heart of Barcelona's tiki-taka generation, alongside Xavi, Iniesta and Puyol. They called him 'El Káiser' at the Camp Nou, where he won four La Liga titles and two Champions Leagues in seven seasons under Rijkaard and Guardiola. He had been a Monaco Ligue 1 winner before that, and started at Atlas in Guadalajara at sixteen. The international shirt is what defined him: five World Cups for Mexico from 2002 to 2018, captain at every one, the only footballer in history to do that. He returns to this World Cup on the bench as Aguirre's assistant and confirmed successor.
footballers.numbers: 3 goals in 19 World Cup matches across five tournaments
didYouKnow: He is the only footballer in history to captain his country at five different World Cup finals (Guinness World Record).
He scored the equaliser against hosts South Africa in the opening match of the 2010 World Cup, denying Bafana Bafana the perfect start.
He is one of only four players to appear at five World Cup tournaments, alongside Antonio Carbajal, Lothar Matthäus and Gianluigi Buffon.

footballers.name: Edin Džeko
clues: I played at the 2014 World Cup, scoring 1 goal in 3 games for my country. I am here again at the 2026 World Cup.
I'm my country's all-time leading goalscorer and most-capped player.
I had a goal controversially disallowed for offside against Nigeria at the 2014 World Cup, my country's first ever appearance at the tournament.
I was Serie A's top scorer in 2016–17 with 29 goals for a club in Rome.
I won the Bundesliga top-scorer trophy in 2009–10, then crossed Europe and won the Premier League twice in three years.
footballers.story: Edin Džeko's family flat was shelled during the Siege of Sarajevo. Bosnia's youth academies rejected him for being too skinny. He left for Teplice in the Czech Republic at nineteen for about €25,000, and quietly became one of Europe's most reliable strikers. Bundesliga top scorer at Wolfsburg, two Premier League titles at Manchester City, Serie A top scorer at Roma at thirty-one, still scoring goals at forty for Schalke. Bosnia & Herzegovina's all-time leading goalscorer and the captain who took them to their first ever World Cup in 2014 and who carries the armband again at the 2026 tournament.
footballers.numbers: 1 goal in 3 World Cup matches at the 2014 tournament
didYouKnow: He scored Bosnia & Herzegovina's only ever World Cup victory, the winner in their 3-1 defeat of Iran at the 2014 tournament.
He came on as a substitute against QPR on the final day of the 2011–12 Premier League season and scored the 92nd-minute equaliser that set up Sergio Agüero's title-winning goal.
He is Bosnia & Herzegovina's all-time leading goalscorer and most-capped player.

footballers.name: Oscar
clues: I played at the 2014 World Cup, scoring 2 goals in 7 games for my country at the tournament they hosted.
I won the Silver Ball at the 2011 FIFA Under-20 World Cup in Colombia, scoring a hat-trick in the final as my country beat Portugal 3-2 from 2-1 down.
I won the Premier League twice in three seasons in London.
I scored my country's only goal in their greatest humiliation against Germany.
I left a London club for an Asian record fee of £60 million in January 2017. The new contract was reported as £400,000 a week.
footballers.story: Oscar was Brazil's golden boy. The São Paulo academy graduate who came through Internacional, lit up the FIFA U-20 World Cup in 2011 with a hat-trick in the final, and was sold to Chelsea at twenty for around £25 million. He won two Premier League titles and the Europa League at Stamford Bridge before, in January 2017 aged twenty-five, taking a £60 million Asian-record move to Shanghai SIPG on £400,000-a-week wages and effectively vanishing from elite European football. The 2014 World Cup at home was bookended by his clipped toe-poke against Croatia in the opener and his lone goal in the 7-1 humiliation by Germany. Retired April 2026, aged thirty-four, after a health problem was detected in pre-season screening.
honours: FIFA U-20 World Cup Silver Ball (Brazil, 2011)
footballers.numbers: 2 goals in 7 World Cup matches at the 2014 tournament
didYouKnow: He scored Brazil's only goal in their 7-1 semi-final defeat to Germany at the 2014 World Cup, the country's worst ever competitive result.
He left Chelsea for Shanghai SIPG in January 2017 for £60 million, the largest fee ever paid for a player in the Chinese Super League at the time.
He retired in April 2026 aged 34 after a heart condition was detected in pre-season screening at São Paulo.

footballers.name: Arjen Robben
clues: I played in three World Cups (2006, 2010 and 2014), scoring 6 goals across 15 games.
I have been fouled more times at the World Cup than any other player since 2006.
I scored the only goal of the 2013 Champions League final in the 89th minute against Borussia Dortmund.
Iker Casillas stuck out a leg to save my one-on-one in the 2010 World Cup final. Andrés Iniesta won it for Spain in extra time.
I'm Dutch. They knew the cut-inside was coming, and they still couldn't stop it.
footballers.story: For fifteen years it was always the same: pick the ball up on the right, cut inside onto the left foot, rifle it into the far corner. Everyone knew it. Nobody stopped it. Arjen Robben won two Premier League titles at Chelsea before he was twenty-three, a La Liga at Real Madrid, then eight Bundesligas at Bayern Munich and the 89th-minute winner in the 2013 Champions League final against Borussia Dortmund. For the Netherlands he played at three World Cups. He came within an Iker Casillas leg of winning the 2010 final in normal time. Iniesta won it in extra time.
footballers.numbers: 6 goals in 15 World Cup matches across three tournaments
didYouKnow: Iker Casillas saved his one-on-one in the 2010 World Cup final. Andrés Iniesta scored the winner for Spain in extra time.
He scored the only goal of the 2013 Champions League final, in the 89th minute against Borussia Dortmund.
He has been fouled more times at the World Cup than any other player since 2006.

footballers.name: Diego Forlán
clues: I played in three World Cups (2002, 2010 and 2014), scoring 6 goals across 10 games.
After my best World Cup, I signed for an Italian giant aged 32 for around €5 million. I scored twice in Serie A all season.
It took me nine months and thirteen Premier League games to score my first goal.
I won the European Golden Shoe twice, with two different Spanish clubs.
I won the Golden Ball as best player at the 2010 World Cup, finishing as joint top scorer on five goals with Müller, Villa and Sneijder.
footballers.story: Diego Forlán took nine months and thirteen Premier League games to score his first Premier League goal at Manchester United, and was widely written off as a Sir Alex misfire. Then he went to Spain. He won the European Golden Shoe at Villarreal in 2004-05, won it again at Atlético Madrid in 2008-09, scored both goals in the 2010 Europa League final, and at the 2010 World Cup he turned thirty-one and was suddenly the best player on Earth. Five goals, the Golden Boot, the Golden Ball, a 30-yard dipping volley against Germany in the third-place playoff voted goal of the tournament. Inter Milan signed him next: two Serie A goals all season. His father played for Uruguay at the 1966 and 1974 World Cups.
footballers.numbers: 6 goals in 10 World Cup matches across three tournaments (5 of them at South Africa 2010)
didYouKnow: He won the Golden Ball as best player at the 2010 World Cup, finishing as joint top scorer on 5 goals with Thomas Müller, David Villa and Wesley Sneijder.
His 30-yard dipping volley against Germany in the third-place playoff was voted the 2010 World Cup's goal of the tournament.
His father Pablo Forlán played for Uruguay at the 1966 and 1974 World Cups.

footballers.name: El Hadji Diouf
clues: I played at the 2002 World Cup, scoring 0 goals in 5 games for my country.
I won the Scottish Premier League and the Scottish League Cup with a famous Glasgow club in 2010–11.
I was named African Footballer of the Year in both 2001 and 2002, back-to-back, with my World Cup in between.
I tormented France's defence in the opening match of the 2002 World Cup, skinning Frank Leboeuf on the byline to set up the goal that beat the reigning world champions 1-0.
I joined Liverpool on the back of my country's heroics for around £10 million. I scored three Premier League goals in two seasons.
footballers.story: The most notorious player on Pelé's FIFA 100 list. El Hadji Diouf was a £10 million Liverpool flop who scored three Premier League goals in two seasons. He was banned for spitting at a Celtic fan from the bench, accused of spitting at a Portsmouth defender and a Middlesbrough ball boy, and was called a 'disgrace' by Sir Alex Ferguson. He was also African Footballer of the Year in both 2001 and 2002. At the 2002 World Cup he tormented Frank Leboeuf on the byline and set up the goal that beat reigning champions France 1-0 in the most iconic upset of the tournament. The drift through Bolton, Sunderland, Blackburn, Rangers, Doncaster and Leeds took him into his forties.
honours: African Footballer of the Year x2 (2001, 2002)
footballers.numbers: 0 goals in 5 World Cup matches at the 2002 tournament
didYouKnow: He set up Papa Bouba Diop's goal as Senegal beat reigning champions France 1-0 in the 2002 World Cup opener, the tournament's most iconic upset.
He was African Footballer of the Year in both 2001 and 2002, back to back.
He was named on Pelé's FIFA 100 list of greatest living footballers in 2004.

footballers.name: James Rodríguez
clues: I played at the 2014 and 2018 World Cups, scoring 6 goals across 8 games for my country. I am here again at the 2026 World Cup as captain.
I have played for clubs on Merseyside, in Munich and in Madrid.

footballers.story: Real Madrid paid roughly €80 million to bring James Rodríguez to Spain in the summer of 2014, days after his World Cup explosion. He was twenty-three. It was the fourth-most-expensive transfer in football history at the time. He scored seventeen across all competitions in his first season under Carlo Ancelotti. Then Zidane arrived, the bench beckoned, the Bayern loan was offered, Bayern declined the buy option. The drift: Everton, Al-Rayyan, Olympiacos, São Paulo, Rayo Vallecano, León. He never recaptured the Brazilian summer he won the Golden Boot in. But here he is, thirty-four years old, captaining Colombia at his third World Cup.
honours: FIFA Club World Cup x2 (Real Madrid)
footballers.numbers: 6 goals in 8 World Cup matches across two tournaments (all 6 at Brazil 2014)
didYouKnow: His swerving left-footed volley against Uruguay in the 2014 round of 16 was voted FIFA Goal of the Tournament.
He won the 2014 World Cup Golden Boot with 6 goals, scoring in every match Colombia played.
Real Madrid signed him for around €80 million days after the 2014 World Cup, the fourth most expensive transfer in history at the time.

footballers.name: Pavel Nedvěd

footballers.story: The Czech Fury. Pavel Nedvěd ran for ninety minutes every game for ninety years. Lazio bought him from Sparta after his stunning Euro 96 (where the Czechs lost the final to Germany on a golden goal), and he won Serie A there in 2000, two Coppa Italias and the Cup Winners' Cup. He moved across to Juventus and won the Ballon d'Or in 2003 by 62 points over Thierry Henry. He stayed at Juve through their forced relegation to Serie B in the 2006 Calciopoli scandal, playing in the second tier with them. Two further Juve Serie A titles were stripped retrospectively. He was later Juventus vice-chairman until he resigned in 2022.
honours: Czech Footballer of the Year x7
footballers.numbers: 0 goals in 3 World Cup matches at the 2006 tournament
didYouKnow: He won the 2003 Ballon d'Or by 62 points over Thierry Henry.
He stayed at Juventus when they were forcibly relegated to Serie B in the 2006 Calciopoli scandal and played in the second tier with them.
He lost the Euro 1996 final to Germany on a golden goal at Wembley.

footballers.name: Tim Howard
clues: I played in two World Cups (2010 and 2014) for my country.
I won the FA Cup and the Community Shield in my first season at my Premier League club.
I have played for a Premier League club in both Manchester and Liverpool.
I made 16 saves in a single World Cup match against Belgium in 2014, the most by any goalkeeper in any World Cup match since records began in 1966.
Sir Alex Ferguson signed me from the New York MetroStars in 2003 to replace Fabien Barthez.
footballers.story: Tim Howard spent a decade at Everton, more than 350 Premier League games as the Toffees' goalkeeper from 2006 to 2016. Before that, Sir Alex Ferguson had signed him from the New York MetroStars in 2003 to replace Fabien Barthez at Manchester United, where he won the FA Cup in his first season before losing his place. The defining game came at the 2014 World Cup in Salvador, where he made sixteen saves over 120 minutes against Belgium, the most by any goalkeeper in any World Cup match since records began in 1966. The United States still lost 2-1 in extra time. He is his country's most-capped goalkeeper ever.
honours: US Soccer National Hall of Fame (2024)
footballers.numbers: 16 saves in a single World Cup match vs Belgium 2014, most by any goalkeeper since records began in 1966
didYouKnow: His 16 saves against Belgium in the 2014 round of 16 are the most by any goalkeeper in a single World Cup match since records began in 1966.
After that game, somebody edited the Wikipedia entry for the US Secretary of Defense to read 'Tim Howard'.
He scored a 100-yard wind-aided goal for Everton against Bolton in January 2012.

footballers.name: Wout Weghorst
clues: I played at the 2022 World Cup, scoring 2 goals in 4 games for my country.
I was booked in the 2022 World Cup before even coming on as a substitute.
In the same game, I scored deep in added time from a clever free-kick routine.
I had a disastrous spell at a Premier League club after a £12 million transfer, in which I only scored 2 goals and we got relegated.
I was signed as an emergency loan for one of the Premier League's biggest clubs under a manager of my own nationality.
footballers.story: A 6'6" striker who started in the Dutch second tier with FC Emmen and didn't make his Eredivisie debut until twenty-one. Wout Weghorst then became one. Fifty-nine Bundesliga goals at Wolfsburg, second only to Robert Lewandowski during his stint. A £12 million transfer to Burnley in January 2022 produced two Premier League goals in twenty appearances as the Clarets were relegated. An emergency loan to Manchester United under fellow Dutchman Erik ten Hag the next season won him his first trophy at thirty, the EFL Cup. At the 2022 World Cup he came off the bench in the quarter-final against Argentina and scored twice in the last twelve minutes to force extra time, a back-post header and then an audacious low free-kick finish in stoppage time. The Netherlands lost on penalties.
footballers.numbers: 2 goals in 4 World Cup matches at the 2022 tournament (both in the QF vs Argentina)
didYouKnow: He came off the bench in the 2022 World Cup quarter-final and scored twice in twelve minutes to force extra time against Argentina, including a header off a low free-kick routine.
Argentina won on penalties, and Messi's 'Andá pa' allá, bobo' to Weghorst in the post-match interview became one of the iconic images of the tournament.
He had scored zero goals in 20 Premier League games for Burnley before that World Cup.

footballers.name: Keisuke Honda
clues: I played in three World Cups (2010, 2014 and 2018), scoring 4 goals across 10 games.
I won the Russian Premier League and two Russian Cups with CSKA Moscow.
I'm the only Asian player with 4 World Cup goals.
I joined AC Milan in 2014 and wore the No. 10 shirt previously worn by Seedorf, Boban and Rui Costa.
I'm Japanese. I won the AFC Asian Cup in 2011 and was named tournament MVP.
footballers.story: Keisuke Honda was Japan's player of the early 2010s. He won the AFC Asian Cup in 2011 as tournament MVP, won the Russian Premier League and two Russian Cups at CSKA Moscow, then signed for AC Milan in 2014 and wore the No. 10 shirt previously worn by Seedorf, Boban and Rui Costa. Three consecutive World Cups, four goals across them, the only Japanese player to score at three different tournaments and the only Asian player with four. His 2010 free-kick against Denmark from thirty yards dipped into the top corner past Thomas Sørensen. His 2018 goal against Senegal made him the first professional footballer to score competitive goals on all six continents. The club career drifted through ten countries and he was briefly general manager of Cambodia while still playing for Botafogo.
footballers.numbers: 4 goals in 10 World Cup matches across three tournaments
didYouKnow: He is the only Japanese player to score at three different World Cups, and the only Asian player with 4 World Cup goals.
His 2018 goal against Senegal made him the first professional footballer to score a competitive goal on all six continents.
He was briefly head coach of Cambodia's national team while still playing club football for Botafogo.

footballers.name: Maxi Rodríguez
clues: I played in three World Cups (2006, 2010 and 2014), scoring 3 goals across 12 games.
I was runner-up in a World Cup final after losing in extra time.
I scored the goal of the tournament at the 2006 World Cup.
I won the League Cup with Liverpool.
